reasoning: This is intentional design. Kernel L4 entries are shared across all address spaces and always treated as present in the resolution logic — the `l4i < self.kernel_l4_end` branch deliberately bypasses the present-bit check because kernel mappings are managed separately and assumed valid.
The ensures clause is just `true`, making this trivially valid regardless of preconditions. It demonstrates no actual spec gap — the preconditions are satisfiable but the property proved is vacuous.

The preconditions are contradictory: `spec_resolve_mapping_1g_l3` checks `!ps` and returns None when ps==false, yet the precondition requires it to be Some while also requiring ps==false. This is vacuously true due to unsatisfiable preconditions, not a real spec issue.
